source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Regular Updates and Patches

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Regular Updates and Patches
input_row.description: Stay informed about the latest security updates and patches released by Amazon for DocumentDB. Regularly apply updates and patches to your DocumentDB instances to protect against known vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Helps the organization reduce their security risk by regularly updating and patching their database and database engine. Regularly updating and scanning for any weaknesses in the company can bring up vulnerabilities that could have led to potential cyber-attack.
input_row.audit: 1. Stay Informed • Stay updated with Amazon DocumentDB announcements, release notes, and security bulletins. • Subscribe to AWS newsletters, forums, and notifications to receive timely updates regarding updates and patches. 2. Plan for Maintenance Windows • Determine a suitable maintenance window to apply updates and patches to your DocumentDB cluster. • Consider the impact on your applications and users when scheduling the maintenance window. 3. Monitor the AWS Management Console • Regularly check the AWS Management Console for notifications related to available updates and patches for your DocumentDB cluster. • The console will provide information on new versions and available patches. 4. Review the Release Notes and Changelog • Before applying any updates or patches, review the release notes and changelog for the new version or patch. • Pay attention to any compatibility or breaking changes that may require application adjustments.  5. Create a Test Environment (Optional) • If feasible, create a separate test environment that closely resembles your production environment. • Deploy a copy of your DocumentDB cluster in the test environment to test the updates and patches before applying them to production. 6. Apply Updates and Patches • During the scheduled maintenance window, initiate the process to apply updates and patches to your DocumentDB cluster. • Follow the recommended procedure provided by AWS, which may involve a few simple clicks in the AWS Management Console. • Ensure that you select the appropriate version or patch to apply. 7. Monitor the Update Process • Monitor the progress of the update or patch application for your DocumentDB cluster. • AWS will provide status updates during the process to keep you informed. 8. Verify Post-Update Functionality • After the update or patch is applied, test the functionality of your applications that rely on the DocumentDB cluster. • Verify that your applications are working as expected and that any integration or dependencies are intact. 9. Review and Update Documentation • Update your documentation, including standard operating procedures (SOPs), to reflect the new version or patch applied to the DocumentDB cluster. • Document any changes or considerations specific to the update or patch. 10. Monitor for New Updates • Continuously monitor for new updates and patches released by AWS for DocumentDB. • Repeat the update process regularly to ensure your DocumentDB cluster remains up to date with the latest security enhancements and bug fixes.
input_row.impact: Helps the organization reduce their security risk by regularly updating and patching their database and database engine. Regularly updating and scanning for any weaknesses in the company can bring up vulnerabilities that could have led to potential cyber-attack.
input_row.justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control centers on administrative processes—staying informed about DocumentDB updates and planning/applying patches—which cannot be fully verified or enforced via AWS APIs, boto3, AWS CLI, or AWS Config. While you can check the current engine version of DocumentDB clusters, there is no API to confirm whether updates have been regularly reviewed/applied in accordance with AWS announcements or organizational patch management policies.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Review the organization's documented process for monitoring Amazon DocumentDB updates, including evidence of subscriptions to AWS announcements, newsletters, or security bulletins. 2. Verify that the organization has a defined and scheduled maintenance window for applying DocumentDB updates and patches. Check change management records or maintenance schedules. 3. In the AWS Console, navigate to Amazon DocumentDB > Clusters. For each cluster, review the engine version and compare it to the latest version announced by AWS (reference AWS release notes). 4. Confirm that updates and patches have been applied in a timely manner by reviewing maintenance logs, change tickets, or other administrative records. 5. Ensure there is a process for assessing the impact of updates and communicating planned maintenance to stakeholders. 6. Document evidence of compliance, including screenshots or export of cluster versions, and records of update/patch application.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Step 1:** Navigate to the AWS Management Console and go to the Amazon DocumentDB service.
2. **Step 2:** Select "Clusters" from the left sidebar and review the list of DocumentDB clusters.
3. **Step 3:** For each cluster, check the "Engine Version" displayed against the latest version announced by AWS. This information can typically be found in the AWS release notes or directly on the DocumentDB product page.
4. **Step 4:** Review the organization's documentation for processes regarding update and patch management. Check for evidence of subscriptions to AWS announcements, newsletters, or security bulletins that notify about updates.
5. **Step 5:** Verify that there is a defined maintenance window for applying updates and patches. Review change management records or any maintenance schedules available to confirm this.
6. **Step 6:** Monitor any maintenance logs, change tickets, or administrative records to confirm whether updates and patches have been applied in a timely manner.
7. **Step 7:** Ensure that there is a documented process for assessing the impact of updates and notifying stakeholders about planned maintenance.
8. **Step 8:** Document all findings, including screenshots or exports of current cluster versions and records showing patch applications.

The process primarily involves human judgment and verification due to the administrative nature of tracking updates and reviews, making it suitable for a manual audit approach.
